title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
description: The lack of information about the system's architecture and resource constraints makes it difficult to assess the risk of denial-of-service attacks.  However, if the system is vulnerable to input-based attacks (as suggested by the data injection threat), an attacker could potentially flood the system with requests, consuming excessive resources and causing a denial of service.
attack_vector: Flooding the system with malicious requests, exploiting vulnerabilities in the application logic.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Utilize a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic.
Employ load balancing and auto-scaling to handle increased traffic volumes.
